---
title: "Introducing Tomat: A Pomodoro Timer for Status Bars"
author: Johan Larsson
date: 2025-11-10
description: |
  I've decided that the world needs yet another pomodoro timer.
  yapm was unfortunately already taken, so I named it
  after the Swedish tomato.
categories:
  - tomat
  - productivity
  - pomodoro
reference-location: margin
image: image.png
title-block-banner: banner.png
---

## Introduction

I have on-and-off been a user of the Pomodoro technique, which
is based on splitting your work day into increments of, typically,
25-minute work sessions followed by 5-minute breaks, repeated
say, four times, and then followed by a longer, 15-minute break.

Since I spend most of my workday in front of my computer, using a 
computer application has seemed to be the most suitable approach.
Unfortunately, there is a jungle of Pomodoro timers out there, and
finding a good one has proven hard. Before I turned to window
managers like i3 and sway,^[Which is what I am currently using.]
I used the [GNOME Pomodoro Timer](https://gnomepomodoro.org/),
which worked out great. Ever since I switched from GNOME, however,
I've yet to find a good replacement.

What I have been looking for, specifically, is a Pomodoro timer
that integrates with my status bar (currently waybar) and that
I can control using either keyboard shortcuts or mouse clicks.

There are alternatives that can be used for this problem,
such as the following:

- [waybar-pomodoro-timer](https://github.com/Andeskjerf/waybar-module-pomodoro)
- [Tomato.C](https://github.com/gabrielzschmitz/Tomato.C)
- [potato-c](https://github.com/nimaaskarian/potato-c)
- [waybar-timer](https://github.com/jbirnick/waybar-timer)
- [uair](https://github.com/metent/uair)

Even though all of these have their own merits, they also
came with problems or s
